questions.explanation: Adopting container technologies provides applications with extreme environmental portability, environment isolation, and precise underlying resource management.

questions.text: A FinTech bank has created a new credit card line; therefore, will need to expand its database to capture new customer profiles and link existing customer profiles to the new card line, while maintaining security and PCI compliance requirements. The bank is entirely in the cloud and provides their customer's with unique financial visualizations about their financial habits. Their customers use a mobile app and website for their account transactions and profiles.
questions.explanation: Amazon Neptune is a fully managed, PCI-compliant graph database optimized for tracking complex data interconnections, such as mapping financial profiles across multiple lines of credit.

questions.text: The automotive industry is now developing a specific unmanned ground vehicle (UGV) to be used by the military that provides alerts to the operator of hazardous conditions, loss of connection, or vehicle damage. They know they will need to align with the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) guidelines 800-171 regarding the vehicle's security strength to protect its location at all times. The application used by the UGV will be hosted in a private subnet.
options: Specify a public subnet for creation
Provide access through relevant security groups
Specify an Elastic IP address for association
Implement redundancy in different Availability Zones
questions.explanation: To allow private instances to contact external services securely, you must instantiate a public subnet containing a NAT Gateway, update target route tables, and associate Elastic IPs for translation.

questions.text: A local hospital is preparing for an audit and needs to show that they are in compliance with HIPAA requirements. This includes security, data lifecycle management, and compliance. They have experienced problems in the past with an employee that has accessed patient records that were not relevant to their immediate job tasks. The CEO of the hospital wants to ensure that mitigation to this issue has been resolved and has had the SysOps team employ various AWS monitoring and security services.
questions.explanation: Standard IAM policy condition blocks track authorization API states using parameters like the explicit policy Version, the query Effect status, and the validation Start Time window.

questions.text:
A non-profit organization has multiple committees that have individual accounts. They are looking at ways to reduce cost, yet ensure that expenses are best utilized across the committees and are finding that individual accounts are no longer working. Since they receive donations, they want to ensure that they can provide use and expense details to their donors when requested.
The Support Specialist has noticed the service control policies (SCPs) in AWS Organizations has exceeded its maximum size. She has begun removing some of the white space characters outside the quotation marks.
What is the maximum size of an SCP document so she can correct the error?
questions.explanation: AWS Organizations enforces a strict maximum document storage constraint limit of exactly 5,120 bytes for every individual Service Control Policy (SCP) JSON file.

questions.text: A global travel company has announced a company restructure in order to re-invest profits into customer facing technology. The IT budget has been re-allocated by 20% for the upcoming year; therefore, staff will be forced to make standardizations and efficiencies with the new budget until the new technology generates increased profits.
options: Coordinating the creation of stack resources
Sending responses to resources within an AWS virtual private cloud (VPC)
Tracking the status of configuration process
Tracking the configuration of Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) instances
questions.explanation: AWS CloudFormation automates configuration infrastructure deployments while generating event state updates to transparently track the setup process status.

questions.text: The local power company has recently installed a new power grid within the community. They are working with the local government to acquire funding for a second power grid, but must show data that shows the cost reductions and how that translates to increased consumption time windows. This data needs to be kept for a minimum of 10 years in case of audit or by inquiry of a government agency. The power company wants to engage the community and gain their support, as their individual and communal power consumption is reflected in the data. The CEO has decided that the company will now begin processing the big data in real time through the use of Amazon Kinesis. The architecture has been developed with, an intermediate and backup Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) bucket for potential delivery failures and source records.
questions.explanation: For complex analytics, real-time Kinesis data is streamed into an Amazon Redshift cluster warehouse for parsing, while raw backups are moved to Amazon S3 Glacier to meet a 10-year audit retention policy.

questions.text: What is the pricing model that enables AWS customers to pay for resources on an as- needed basis? (Select the best answer.)
questions.explanation: The pay-as-you-go model allows operational cost flexibility, letting users adapt to changing business needs without overcommitting budgets.

questions.text: All are a cloud deployment model EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS), Platform as a Service (PaaS), and Software as a Service (SaaS) are the three primary standard cloud computing service types.

questions.text: True or False? AWS owns and maintains the network-connected hardware required for application services, while you provision and use what you need.
questions.explanation: Under the cloud utility framework, AWS manages physical facilities and network infrastructure assets while customers build logical systems on top.

questions.text: Which of these is not a primary benefit of cloud computing over on-premises computing? Select the best answer.
options: Eliminate guessing on your infrastructure capacity needs
Benefit from massive economies of scale
Pay for racking, stacking, and powering servers
questions.explanation: Eliminating costly physical maintenance burdens like racking, stacking, and power delivery is a core value proposition of shifting off on-premises setups.

questions.text: All are benefits of AWS Cloud computing EXCEPT (Choose two):
questions.explanation: Cloud computing replaces long corporate hardware procurement cycles with fast API calls and provides high availability with low network latency thresholds.

questions.text: What is a reason businesses are switching to cloud computing?
questions.explanation: Cloud elasticity grants technology teams the capability to spin up resources instantly, minimizing time-to-market constraints and eliminating over-provisioning waste.

questions.text: True or False? Cloud computing provides a simple way to access servers, storage, databases, and a broad set of application services over the internet. You own the network-connected hardware required for these services and AWS provisions what you need.
questions.explanation: This statement is false because the cloud provider (AWS) owns and maintains the physical data center hardware assets, not the end customer.

questions.text: Economies of scale result from ___. Select the best answer.
options: having to invest heavily in data centers and servers
having hundreds of thousands of customers aggregated in the cloud
having hundreds of cloud services available over the internet
questions.explanation: Aggregating usage across hundreds of thousands of active customer nodes allows AWS to achieve massive bulk discounts and regularly pass structural cost savings back to users.

questions.text: What are the three ways to access AWS services? Select three responses.
options: AWS Command Line Interface AWS CLI
questions.explanation: The management console web interface, programmatic API command terminals (CLI), and language runtimes (SDKs) form the primary interaction layers for AWS services.

questions.text: Which category of service does Amazon Lightsail belong to?
questions.explanation: Amazon Lightsail provides simplified pre-configured virtual private servers, storage, and databases, acting as a classic cloud Platform as a Service (PaaS) solution.

questions.text: True or False? You can launch multiple instances of different types from a single Amazon Machine Image (AMI) when you launch an Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) instance.
questions.explanation: An Amazon Machine Image (AMI) provides a software configuration template from which you can launch many distinct virtual machine instance types.

questions.text: If your project requires you to run monthly reports that iterate through very large amounts of data, which Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) purchasing option should you consider?
questions.explanation: Scheduled Reserved Instances allow you to purchase capacity reservations that recur predictably at specific times, making them ideal for tasks like predictable monthly report generations.

questions.text: Which Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) feature ensures your instances will not share a physical host with instances from any other AWS customer?
questions.explanation: Dedicated Instances are EC2 virtual servers that run in an isolated VPC on physical host hardware reserved exclusively for a single AWS account holder.

questions.text: With the "Pay as you go" pricing model, what is the billing interval for compute resources from the time you launch a resource until you terminate it?
questions.explanation: AWS compute resource pricing operates dynamically; depending on the OS platform, you are billed on an hourly or per-second interval for the precise duration resources run.

questions.text: Your web application needs four instances to support steady traffic all the time. On the last day of the month, the traffic triples. What is the most cost-effective way to handle this pattern?
options: Run four Reserved Instances constantly, then add eight On-Demand Instances on the last day of each month.
Run four On-Demand Instances constantly, then add eight more On-Demand Instances on the last day of each month.
Run 12 Reserved Instances all of the time.
Run four On-Demand Instances consistently, then add eight Reserved Instances on the last day of each month.
questions.explanation: Reserved Instances provide heavy cost discounts for predictable baseline workloads (4 instances), while On-Demand instances handle elastic demand spikes cost-effectively.

questions.text: Which of the following must be specified when launching a new Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) Windows instance? Select two responses.
questions.explanation: Launching an EC2 instance fundamentally requires selecting the virtual operating system image template (AMI) and the hardware capacity size footprint (Instance Type).

questions.text: Why is AWS more economical than traditional data centers for applications with varying compute workloads? Select the best answer.
options: Customers can permanently run enough instances to handle peak workloads.
Customers retain full administrative access to their Amazon EC2 instances
Amazon EC2 costs are billed on a monthly basis.
Amazon EC2 instances can be launched on-demand when needed.
questions.explanation: On-demand elasticity allows you to deploy hardware capacity instantly when user traffic spikes and turn it off when load subsides, preventing data center infrastructure waste.

questions.text: For certain services like Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) and Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS), you can invest in reserved capacity. Reserved Instances are available in what three payment options? (Select THREE.)
questions.explanation: AWS Reserved Instances provide flexibility by offering three distinct payment options: No Up-front, Partial Up-front, and All Up-front.

questions.text: What type of AWS data transfer is free for Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3)? (Select THREE.)
options: Up to 1 GB data transfer OUT from Amazon S3 to the internet
Outbound data transfer from Amazon S3 to Amazon CloudFront
Outbound data transfer from Amazon S3 to all Regions
Data transfer IN to Amazon S3 from Internet
questions.explanation: AWS does not charge for inbound data transfers from the internet, outbound transfers from S3 to CloudFront edge caches, or standard usage within the free tier limits.

questions.text: True or false? To receive the discounted rate associated with Reserved Instances, you must make a full, up-front payment for the term of the agreement.
questions.explanation: Reserved Instances allow you to choose a 'No Up-front' or 'Partial Up-front' payment plan and still receive a discounted hourly rate compared to On-Demand pricing.

questions.text: There is no charge for which of the following? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: As a baseline billing rule, inbound data transfer from the internet is free, and transferring data between internal services within the same availability zone incurs no network cost.

questions.text: How often do you pay for compute resources from the time you launch a resource until you terminate it with the "pay as you go" pricing model?
questions.explanation: Under the pay-as-you-go model, operational compute resources are billed flexibly based on actual usage fractions calculated by the hour or by the second.

questions.text: As AWS grows, the general cost of doing business is reduced and savings are passed back to the customer in the form of lower pricing. What is this optimization called?
questions.explanation: Economies of scale occur when aggregating massive quantities of customer infrastructure allows AWS to procure hardware at bulk discounts and lower prices for everyone.

questions.text: True or false? AWS offers a variety of services at no charge. For example, Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC), AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM), consolidated billing, AWS Elastic Beanstalk, AWS Auto Scaling, AWS OpsWorks and AWS CloudFormation. Although there is no charge for these services, there may be charges associated with other AWS services used in conjunction with these services.
questions.explanation: Many core provisioning tools carry no service fee, but you remain responsible for the underlying compute instances, storage volumes, or databases they create.

questions.text: When are free AWS data transfers applicable? (Select TWO.)
options: Free inbound data transfer across all AWS services in all Regions
Free outbound data transfer across all AWS services in all Regions
Free outbound data transfers within the same Region, within the same Availability Zone, and that use a private IP address
Free inbound data transfer for Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) instances
questions.explanation: Inbound data transfers are free, and outbound network traffic is unbilled if it stays within the same region and availability zone while utilizing private IP space.

questions.text: True or false? Unlimited services are available via the AWS Free Tier to new AWS customers for 12 months following their AWS signup date.
questions.explanation: The 12-Month Free Tier limits resource consumption to specific service quotas rather than offering unlimited free usage.

questions.text: Which component of the AWS global infrastructure does Amazon CloudFront use to ensure low-latency delivery?
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudFront relies on a global network of AWS Edge Locations to cache and distribute copies of content closer to international users, drastically minimizing latency.

questions.text: You can run applications and workloads from a Region to ___ latency to end users.
questions.explanation: Deploying your architecture workloads inside an AWS Region physically located near your primary target audience allows you to significantly decrease round-trip network latency.

questions.text: True or False? Each Availability Zone is designed to be isolated from failures in other Availability Zones.
questions.explanation: Every Availability Zone is engineered within distinct physical structures with independent power utility grids and cooling loops to isolate physical failure hazards from neighboring zones.

questions.text: Which of the following are geographic areas that host two or more Availability Zones?
questions.explanation: An AWS Region represents a distinct, isolated geographic footprint worldwide that encapsulates a minimum cluster of two or more interconnected Availability Zones.

questions.text: ___ means the infrastructure has built in component redundancy and ___ means that resources dynamically adjust to increases or decreases in capacity requirements.
options: Elastic and scalable, no human intervention
questions.explanation: Fault tolerance signifies structural component redundancy capable of absorbing hardware failure seamlessly, while elasticity defines dynamic resource capacity adjustment under volatile workloads.

questions.text: True or False? Availability Zones within a Region are connected through low-latency links.
questions.explanation: Availability Zones situated within the same corporate Region link directly to each other through redundant, ultra-fast fiber optic network lines.

questions.text: Which of these statements about Availability Zones is not true? Select the best answer.
options: A data center can be used for more than one Availability Zone.
Availability Zones are connected to each other using high-speed private links.
Availability Zones are designed for fault isolation.
Availability Zones are made up of one or more data centers.
questions.explanation: An Availability Zone can encapsulate multiple distinct physical data center locations, but a single physical data center facility can never be shared across separate Availability Zones.

questions.text: What is true about Regions? Select two responses.
options: Each region is located in a separate geographic area.
Regions are the physical locations of your customers.
Each region is a physical location that has multiple Availability Zones.
All regions are located in one specific geographic area.
questions.explanation: AWS Regions are completely isolated geographic territories containing multiple cluster hubs of Availability Zones designed to house application backends close to specific markets.

questions.text: AWS highly recommends provisioning your compute resources across ___ Availability Zones. Select the best answer.
questions.explanation: AWS architecture frameworks dictate provisioning redundant environment compute targets across multiple Availability Zones to secure structural high availability and system resilience.

questions.text: True or False? Edge locations are only located in the same general area as Regions.
questions.explanation: Edge locations operate independently from the main data centers and are distributed globally across major metropolitan populations far away from standard parent AWS Regions.

questions.text: In the shared responsibility model, AWS has the responsibility of providing what?
questions.explanation: Under the shared responsibility model, AWS is strictly accountable for the 'security of the cloud,' which includes the physical infrastructure, virtualization software layer, and global networking.

questions.text: In the shared responsibility model, which of the following are examples of "security in the cloud?" (Select TWO.)
options: Which AWS services are used with the content
Compliance with compute security standards and regulations
Physical security of the facilities where the services operate
In which country content is stored
questions.explanation: The customer owns 'security in the cloud,' meaning they control data storage locations, access permissions, network settings, and how specific AWS services are deployed.

questions.text: Which of the following are some of the security benefits that AWS offers? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: AWS delivers structural security advantages by offering a globally protected infrastructure footprint alongside automated configurations designed to satisfy stringent third-party compliance standards.

questions.text: True or False? AWS is responsible for the security of everything above the hypervisor layer.
questions.explanation: AWS maintains accountability up to the virtualization hypervisor layer; the customer assumes full operational responsibility for configuring the guest operating system and applications loaded on top.

questions.text: Which of the following is the responsibility of AWS under the shared responsibility model?
questions.explanation: AWS manages physical data center facility assets and bare-metal service hardware maintenance, while shifting configuration access rules and data security parameters to the customer.

questions.text: Which resource provides trusted users with temporary security credentials that can control access to your AWS resources?
options: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) User
questions.explanation: AWS STS is a web service framework designed to issue temporary, short-lived security credentials to authorized users, applications, or federated roles dynamically.

questions.text: Which of the following are AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) best practices? (Select TWO.)
options: Leave unused and unnecessary users and credentials in place.
Provide users with default administrative privileges.
Monitor activity in your AWS account.
Do not use groups to assign permissions to IAM users; provide each user with their own set of custom permissions.
questions.explanation: Core identity management best practices dictate enforcing credential rotation rules, conducting regular audit loops, and limiting user access strictly according to the principle of least privilege.

questions.text: True or False? AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) is appropriate for operating system (OS) and application authentication.
questions.explanation: IAM handles identity and programmatic permission boundaries for AWS cloud resource APIs, not internal OS-level user accounts or independent in-app database authentication logins.

questions.text: Where can a customer find information about prohibited actions on AWS infrastructure?
questions.explanation: The AWS Acceptable Use Policy specifies absolute constraints regarding prohibited behavior, vulnerability testing parameters, and network abuse boundaries on their cloud architecture.

questions.text: True or False? The access key and secret access key are used to log into the AWS Management Console.
questions.explanation: Long-lived access key IDs and secret access keys authenticate programmatic API calls via the CLI or SDKs, whereas interactive console logins require standard passwords and MFA tokens.

questions.text: True or False? Using AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) groups is the recommended way to manage the permission of IAM users by job function.
questions.explanation: Assigning permissions to IAM Groups matching distinct business job functions simplifies access administration and eliminates the security risks of hardcoding individual policies to separate users.

questions.text: After initial login, what does AWS recommend as the best practice for the AWS account root user?
options: Revoke all permissions on the root user account
Restrict permission on the root user account
questions.explanation: AWS strongly recommends deleting all long-lived access keys associated with the account root user immediately after creation to mitigate credential exposure risks.

questions.text: How would a system administrator add an additional layer of login security to a user's access to the AWS Management Console?
options: Audit AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) roles
questions.explanation: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) mandates that console users present a secondary dynamic token validation factor during authorization, mitigating the risk of compromised passwords.

questions.text: Which of the following are advantages of AWS Cloud security? (Select TWO.)
options: AWS infrastructure security auditing is periodic and manual.
AWS uses single-factor access control systems.
You retain complete control and ownership of your data Region.
AWS retains complete control and ownership of your data Region.
AWS uses multi-factor access control systems.
questions.explanation: AWS cloud infrastructure security grants users complete ownership and regional sovereignty over their assets while providing multi-factor internal access control mechanisms.

questions.text: What is AWS Trusted Advisor?
options: A Professional Services offering hat helps you migrate to the cloud.
A partner program that helps you validate your application deployment.
An online tool that helps you configure resources to follow best practices.
An AWS service that helps you manage access to your account.
questions.explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor functions as an optimization engine that continuously scans resource settings against cloud architecture standards across cost, performance, and security categories.

questions.text: #### 16.Which of the following are included in AWS Assurance Programs? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: AWS Assurance Programs provide verification through independent compliance certifications, official security attestations, legal frameworks, and regional regulatory alignments.

questions.text: When you create an AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy, what are the two types of access that can be granted to a user? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: IAM permissions support interactive Management Console entry via standard credential fields or programmatic endpoint access driven by API keys, CLI, and SDK runtime operations.

questions.text: The AWS risk and compliance program is made up of which of the following components? (Select THREE.)
questions.explanation: The structural AWS risk and compliance framework is built on three core pillars: proactive risk management loops, comprehensive information security policies, and an auditing controlled environment.

questions.text: Which AWS networking service enables a company to create a virtual network within AWS? Select the best answer.
questions.explanation: Amazon VPC allows you to provision a logically isolated section of the AWS cloud where you can launch resources in a virtual network you define.

questions.text: What is the minimum size subnet you can have in an Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC)?
questions.explanation: The smallest subnet allowed in an Amazon VPC is a /28 block, which provides 16 total IP addresses (11 usable after AWS reservations).

questions.text: What is the maximum IPv4 address range size you can have in an Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC)?
questions.explanation: The maximum allowed size for a primary or secondary IPv4 CIDR block in an Amazon VPC is /16, providing up to 65,536 total IP addresses.

questions.text: You must allow resources in a private subnet to access the internet. Which of the following provides this capability?
questions.explanation: A NAT Gateway enables resources in a private subnet to connect outbound to the internet while preventing the internet from initiating inbound connections with those instances.

questions.text: True or False? Private subnets have direct access to the internet.
questions.explanation: Private subnets lack a route to an Internet Gateway (IGW), meaning they cannot directly send or receive traffic from the public internet.

questions.text: Which component of the AWS global infrastructure does Amazon CloudFront use to ensure low-latency delivery? Select the best answer.
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudFront utilizes a worldwide network of edge locations to cache and serve content closer to end users, minimizing latency.

questions.text: Which of the following is an optional security control that can be applied at the subnet layer of a VPC?
questions.explanation: Network Access Control Lists (NACLs) operate as a stateless firewall layer checking traffic at the boundary of the subnet, rather than the individual instance level.

questions.text: What happens when you create a new virtual private cloud (VPC) with Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC)? Select the best answer.
options: A main route table is created by default.
Three subnets are created by default, with one subnet for each Availability Zone.
Three subnets are created by default in one Availability Zone.
An internet gateway is created by default.
questions.explanation: When a new custom VPC is created, AWS automatically provisions a default main route table, a default network ACL, and a default security group.

questions.text: How many internet gateways (IGWs) can you attach to an Amazon VPC at any one time?
questions.explanation: An Amazon VPC can have exactly one Internet Gateway (IGW) attached to it at any single point in time.

questions.text: You are a solutions architect who works for a large retail company that is migrating its existing infrastructure to AWS. You recommend they use a custom Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC). They will need a public subnet for their web servers and a private subnet for their databases. They require web servers and database servers to be highly available, with a minimum of two web servers and two database servers each.
questions.explanation: To satisfy high availability across two public web servers and two private database servers, the layout requires duplicating subnets over two unique Availability Zones (4 total subnets).

questions.text: What is used to divide an Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC)?
questions.explanation: Subnets are the core architectural component used to split a larger VPC CIDR block into smaller, isolated logical network segments within an Availability Zone.

questions.text: What defines a subnet?
questions.explanation: A subnet's size and address bounds are strictly defined by its designated Classless Inter-Domain Routing (CIDR) block configuration.

questions.text: How many IP addresses in a subnet are reserved by AWS?
questions.explanation: AWS reserves the first four IP addresses and the very last IP address of every subnet block (5 total) for internal networking, routing, and DNS administration functions.

questions.text: Which statement describes an elastic network interface?
options: A static IPv4 address designed for dynamic cloud computing.
Enables the creation of a private connection between your Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC) without requiring access over the internet or through a
A virtual network interface that you can attach to an instance in an Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC).
An Amazon managed resource designed to operate like a network address translation (NAT) instance, but is simpler to manage and is highly available within an Availability Zone.
questions.explanation: An Elastic Network Interface (ENI) represents a logical virtual network card component that can be created, configured, and bound dynamically to an EC2 instance.

questions.text: True or False. Within a Region, you can create multiple Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC) and have complete control of the network configuration for each VPC.
questions.explanation: AWS allows you to build multiple distinct VPC networks within a single Region, granting absolute configuration authority over subnets, routing, and gateway parameters for each.

questions.text: True or False? Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) is an object storage suitable for the storage of flat files like Microsoft Word documents, photos, and so on.
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 is a cloud-native object storage service designed to store and manage unstructured flat files as discrete object key-value payloads.

questions.text: Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) replicates all objects ___.
options: in multiple Availability Zones within the same Region
on multiple volumes within an Availability Zone
across multiple Regions for higher durability
questions.explanation: To guarantee 99.999999999% durability, Amazon S3 automatically creates redundant baseline copies of your objects across at least three physical Availability Zones inside the target Region.

questions.text: Which of the following can be used as a storage class for an Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) object lifecycle policy? (Select THREE.)
questions.explanation: S3 Standard, S3 Infrequent Access (IA), and Amazon S3 Glacier represent distinct architectural storage classes supported within automated lifecycle tier transition rule loops.

questions.text: The name of an Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) bucket must be unique ___.
options: across all of your AWS accounts
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 uses a global namespace structure, meaning every bucket name must be globally unique worldwide across all active corporate and individual AWS customer accounts.

questions.text: What can you use Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) for with your web application?
options: Store static content such as images, video, CSS, JavaScript, and so on
Host a robust content delivery network (CDN) to deliver entire websites with dynamic, static, and streaming content
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 is optimized to host immutable assets like videos, CSS layouts, images, and HTML wrappers, while compute backends handle processing tasks.

questions.text: Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) is recommended when data ___. (Select TWO.)
options: must be stored in a different Availability Zone than the one the Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) instance is in
must be quickly accessible, requiring long-term persistence
questions.explanation: Amazon EBS provides block-level persistent storage media volumes that support fast block updates, high read/write IOPS performance, and seamless internal KMS volume encryption patterns.

questions.text: True or False? By default, all data stored in Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) is viewable by the public.
questions.explanation: By default, the AWS implicit root configuration enforces private visibility restrictions on newly provisioned S3 storage buckets, blocking public web traffic.

questions.text: Regarding Amazon Simple Storage Service Glacier, what is a vault?
options: An object (photos, videos, files, or documents)
A policy that identifies who can access content stored in Amazon S3 Glacier.
The rules that determine who can (or cannot) access archives
questions.explanation: In Amazon S3 Glacier storage pools, a vault represents a secure workspace container block used to aggregate, manage, and audit long-term archive collections.

questions.text: True or False? Access control lists are used to make entire buckets public, like one hosting a website on Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3).
questions.explanation: Bucket policies, rather than granular Access Control Lists (ACLs), are the standard modern mechanism used to apply broad bucket-wide read permissions for public static website hosting layouts.

questions.text: Which of the following are features of Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS)? (Select TWO.)
options: Amazon EBS data is automatically backed up to tape.
Amazon EBS volumes can be encrypted transparently to workloads on the attached instance.
Data on an Amazon EBS volume is lost when the attached instance is stopped.
Amazon EBS is automatically replicated within an Availability Zone.
questions.explanation: Amazon EBS block volumes offer persistent encryption options and automatically replicate data blocks inside their target Availability Zone to prevent unexpected hardware module failures from causing data loss.

questions.text: You are designing an ecommerce web application that will scale to hundreds of thousands of concurrent users. Which database technology is best suited to hold the session state in this example?
options: NoSQL database table using Amazon DynamoDB
Relational database using Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)
questions.explanation: Amazon DynamoDB provides the single-digit millisecond latency and horizontal scaling required to manage session state for massive volumes of concurrent users.

questions.text: You need to find an item in an Amazon DynamoDB table using an attribute other than the item's primary key. Which of the following operations should you use?
questions.explanation: A Scan operation examines every item in a DynamoDB table sequentially, making it necessary when filtering by non-key attributes without an index.

questions.text: Which AWS Cloud service is best suited for online analytic processing (OLAP)?
questions.explanation: Amazon Redshift is a fast, fully managed data warehouse optimized for complex analytical queries and Online Analytical Processing (OLAP) workloads.

questions.text: In Amazon DynamoDB, an attribute is ___
questions.explanation: In DynamoDB's data model, an attribute represents a fundamental data element that cannot or does not need to be broken down any further.

questions.text: When you create a table in Amazon DynamoDB, in addition to the table name, you must specify the ___ of the table.
questions.explanation: Every DynamoDB table must have its primary key defined at creation time to uniquely identify items and distribute data across physical partitions.

questions.text: ___ is up to five times faster than standard MySQL databases and three times faster than standard PostgreSQL databases.
questions.explanation: Amazon Aurora is a cloud-native relational engine built for RDS that delivers up to 5x the throughput of standard MySQL and 3x of standard PostgreSQL.

questions.text: If you are developing an application that requires a database with extremely fast ___ performance, fast scalability, and flexibility in the database schema, which database should you choose?
questions.explanation: Amazon DynamoDB is a NoSQL engine designed for single-digit millisecond performance, seamless throughput scaling, and schema flexibility.

questions.text: True or False? Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS) automatically patches the database software and backs up your database, storing the backups for a user-defined retention period and enabling point-in-time recovery.
questions.explanation: As a managed service, Amazon RDS handles time-consuming administrative tasks like automated software patching, database backups, and point-in-time recovery tracking.

questions.text: What are the four support plans offered by AWS? Choose four answers.
questions.explanation: AWS offers four progressive support plan tiers to meet different operational scopes: Basic, Developer, Business, and Enterprise.

questions.text: What are the characteristics of the Developer Support Plan? Select two responses.
options: One primary contact can open a case
Unlimited contacts can open a case
Assigned to a Technical Account Manager (TAM)
24/7 access to Cloud Support Engineers via email, chat, and phone
Business hours access to Cloud Support Associates via email
questions.explanation: The Developer plan allows only one primary contact to submit architecture questions via email, with response windows limited to local business hours.

questions.text: Which of the following is not available in the Business Support Plan? Select the best answer.
options: Access to Cloud Support Engineers for technical issues
Access to a Well-Architected review delivered by AWS Solutions Architects
Access to the Personal Health Dashboard and the AWS Health API
questions.explanation: Well-Architected reviews delivered directly by AWS Solutions Architects are reserved for Enterprise-level customer accounts, not the standard Business plan.

questions.text: Which of the AWS Support levels offers 24/7 support via phone or chat? Select two responses.
questions.explanation: Both the Business and Enterprise support plans provide customers with full 24/7 access to Cloud Support Engineers via phone, text chat, or web console.

questions.text: Your company's IT management team is looking for an online tool to provide recommendations to save money, improve system availability and performance, and to help close security gaps. What tool can help the management team?
questions.explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor runs automated optimization checks across your account to evaluate cost savings, security baseline posture, fault tolerance, and performance metrics.

questions.text: AWS Support has five different severity levels for support cases. What are they? Select five responses.
questions.explanation: AWS Support tickets are categorized into five precise case severity impact tiers: Low, Normal, High, Urgent, and Critical.

questions.text: What is the response time of urgent issues for Enterprise Support Plan customers? Select the best answer.
questions.explanation: Enterprise Support tier SLAs guarantee a rapid response time threshold of 1 hour or less for business-critical, urgent-severity system disruptions.

questions.text: A Basic Support Plan allows how many support cases per month? Select the best answer.
questions.explanation: The free Basic Support Plan provides zero technical or architecture case allocations, limiting users strictly to billing queries and service limit adjustments.

questions.text: What will AWS Trusted Advisor tell you? Select three responses.
options: Whether or not you are using fault tolerance
How well the infrastructure is performing
Deployed resources that are not being used, but are incurring charges
Assess the risk of a single Region deployment
When security groups have been closed
questions.explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor monitors active metrics to flag idle resources costing money, detect single-point infrastructure hazards, and review cluster baseline efficiency.

questions.text: Which AWS Support plan includes the assistance of a Technical Account Manager (TAM)? Select the best answer.
questions.explanation: A designated Technical Account Manager (TAM) is a dedicated engineering advisor exclusively assigned to accounts operating on the Enterprise Support Plan tier.

questions.text: Which of the following is not one of the four areas of the performance efficiency pillar in the Well-Architected Framework? Select the best answer.
questions.explanation: Traceability is a core design principle of the Security pillar, not the Performance Efficiency pillar which relies on Selection, Review, Monitoring, and Tradeoffs.

questions.text: Which is not a characteristic of the Amazon EC2 Scaling service? Select the best answer.
options: Responds to changing conditions by adding or terminating Amazon Elastic
Launches instances from a specified Amazon Machine Image (AMI)
Enforces a minimum number of running Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) instances
questions.explanation: Sending traffic to healthy instances is a primary function of Elastic Load Balancing (ELB), rather than a direct feature of the EC2 Auto Scaling service.

questions.text: Which options will help increase the availability of a web server farm? Select two responses.
options: Use Amazon CloudFront to deliver content to end users with low latency.
Deploy the instances in an Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC).
Launch the web server instance across multiple Availability Zones.
Add more CPU and RAM to each instance.
Leverage Scaling to recover from failed instances.
questions.explanation: Deploying EC2 instances across multiple Availability Zones prevents single-point failure, while Auto Scaling automatically substitutes unhealthy nodes to keep the farm operational.

questions.text: Which design principles are recommended when considering performance efficiency? Select two responses.
questions.explanation: Performance efficiency design principles recommend utilizing serverless options to offload management overhead and democratizing complex technologies by consuming them as managed cloud services.

questions.text: Which AWS offering enables customers to find, buy, and immediately start using software solutions in their AWS environment? Select the best answer.
questions.explanation: AWS Marketplace is a digital catalog that allows organizations to find, test, purchase, and deploy third-party software and container services instantly.

questions.text: In a physical data center, security is typically considered in what area? Select the best answer
questions.explanation: Traditional on-premises architecture primarily enforces security protections at the physical perimeter boundaries, making it difficult to scale internal logical access control vectors.

questions.text: To view all categories of instance metadata from within a running instance, which URL should you use? Select the best answer
questions.explanation: AWS reserves the link-local unicast IP address 169.254.169.254 locally to allow active EC2 instances to safely fetch internal instance metadata strings.

questions.text: What is defined as the ability for a system to remain operational even if some of the components of that system fail? Select the best answer.
questions.explanation: Fault tolerance represents the infrastructure state where an environment can sustain a major component degradation without suffering application downtime.

questions.text: Which best describes a system that is always available, without the need for human intervention? Select the best answer.
questions.explanation: High availability systems maximize uptime service level agreements automatically by routing around isolated application node failures without needing operator intervention.

questions.text: Which of the following AWS tools help your application scale up or down based on demand? Select two responses.
questions.explanation: Auto Scaling dynamically adjusts EC2 capacity based on load metrics, while Elastic Load Balancing distributes traffic across those scaling instances.

questions.text: Which service would you use to send alerts based on Amazon CloudWatch alarms? Select the best answer.
questions.explanation: Amazon SNS is a pub/sub messaging service used to send notifications (SMS, email) automatically triggered by CloudWatch alarms.

questions.text: Which is not a characteristic of the Amazon EC2 Scaling service? Select the best answer.
options: Responds to changing conditions by adding or terminating Amazon Elastic
Enforces a minimum number of running Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) instances
Launches instances from a specified Amazon Machine Image (AMI)
questions.explanation: Sending traffic to healthy instances is a core feature of Elastic Load Balancing (ELB), not the Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling service.

questions.text: Which of the following must be configured on an Elastic Load Balancing load balancer to accept incoming traffic?
questions.explanation: A listener is a process that checks for connection requests using configured protocols and ports to route traffic to registered targets.

questions.text: What are the minimum elements required to create an Auto Scaling launch configuration. Select three responses.
questions.explanation: Creating an Auto Scaling launch configuration fundamentally requires an AMI choice, an instance type hardware footprint, and a template name string.

questions.text: How long does Amazon CloudWatch keep metric data? Select the best answer.
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudWatch retains metric data parameters historically for up to 15 months on a rolling schedule for trend analysis.

questions.text: Amazon CloudWatch supports which types of monitoring plans? Select two responses.
options: Basic monitoring, which has an additional cost
Ad hoc monitoring, which has an additional cost
Detailed monitoring, which has an additional cost
questions.explanation: AWS offers standard basic monitoring for free at 5-minute intervals and detailed monitoring at an extra cost for 1-minute intervals.

questions.text: Which of the following is a type of Elastic Load Balancing health check? (Select THREE.)
options: An Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) instance status check.
questions.explanation: ELB health checks evaluate target status actively using basic network level pings, connection attempts, or explicit web page requests.

questions.text: What are the three types of load balancers that Amazon Elastic Load Balancing offers?
questions.explanation: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing provides Application Load Balancers (Layer 7), Network Load Balancers (Layer 4), and legacy Classic Load Balancers.
